jsDoc.doc:
Represents a Strava activity.

This class encapsulates the data for a Strava activity and provides convenient methods for accessing and manipulating
that data.
Constructs a new `Activity` instance.

tags.name: data
tags.doc: The raw activity data from the Strava API. This can be either a summary or a detailed representation.

tags.name: opts
tags.doc: Options for the activity.

tags.name: opts.aliases
tags.doc: A map of segment name aliases.

tags.name: opts.segmentProvider
tags.doc: A provider for retrieving segment data.

jsDoc.doc:
Updates the activity data with new information.

This is useful when you have a summary activity and want to update it with detailed information.

tags.name: data
tags.doc: The new activity data.

jsDoc.doc:
Extracts the IANA timezone name from the Strava timezone string.
Strava format: "(GMT±HH:MM) IANA/Timezone_Name"

tags.doc: The IANA timezone name (e.g., "America/Chicago") or undefined

jsDoc.doc: The start date of the activity as a `DateTime` object with timezone set.
Returns a string representation of the activity, including the date, type, distance, and name.
The geographical coordinates of the activity with optional altitude and time data.
The name of the activity.
The unique identifier of the activity.
The moving time of the activity in seconds.
The elapsed time of the activity in seconds.
The distance of the activity in meters.
The distance of the activity in kilometers, rounded to two decimal places.
The total elevation gain of the activity in meters.
The average temperature of the activity in degrees Celsius.
The name of the device used to record the activity.
Indicates if the activity was marked as a commute.
The ID of the gear used for the activity.
The start date of the activity as a `DateTime` object (UTC).

tags.doc: Use `startDateAsDateTime` instead

jsDoc.doc: The start datetime of the activity in the local timezone, in ISO 8601 format.
The start date in the local timezone in YYYY-MM-DD format.

Creates a timezone-aware DateEx object for a specific time during the activity.

Converts a time offset (in seconds from activity start) to a DateEx object with the
activity's local timezone. Used for generating timezone-aware timestamps in GPX/KML output.

tags.name: delta
tags.doc: Time offset in seconds from activity start (e.g., from stream time data)
DateEx object set to the activity's timezone

jsDoc.doc: The segment efforts associated with the activity.
Sets the segment efforts for the activity.

tags.name: segments
tags.doc: Array of segment effort data

jsDoc.doc: The type of the activity (e.g., 'Ride', 'Run').
Checks if the activity is a ride or an e-bike ride.

Checks if the activity has track points.

Some activity types, such as workouts, yoga, and weight training, do not have KML data.
Checks if the activity has way points.

Extracts custom properties from the activity description.

Custom properties are key-value pairs embedded in the activity description, with each pair on a new line in the
format `key=value`. Any lines that do not match this format are considered part of the description.

tags.doc: A dictionary of custom properties.

jsDoc.doc: Returns the segment efforts for the activity.

tags.doc: An array of segment efforts.

jsDoc.doc:
Fetches coordinate stream data from the Strava API for this activity.

Retrieves one or more stream types (lat/lng, altitude, time, etc.) from Strava and
combines them into TrackPoint objects. The returned data populates the activity's
coordinates array for use in GPX/KML generation.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: Application context with logging

tags.name: streamTypes
tags.doc: Array of stream types to fetch (e.g., LatLng, Altitude, Time)

jsDoc.doc:
Filters the activity's coordinates to remove blackout zones and duplicate points.

Performs two filtering operations:
1. **Blackout zones**: Removes any coordinates that fall within specified rectangular regions
2. **Deduplication**: Removes intermediate points where consecutive coordinates have identical lat/lng

The filtering is done in-place, modifying the activity's coordinates array. Original array
indices (e.g., from lap.start_index) become invalid after filtering.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: Application context with logging

tags.name: dedup
tags.doc: Whether to remove intermediate duplicate coordinates

tags.name: blackoutZones
tags.doc: Array of rectangular regions to exclude (e.g., home locations)

jsDoc.doc:
Fetches detailed activity data from the Strava API and updates this activity.

Upgrades a summary activity to a detailed activity by fetching additional fields such as:
- segment_efforts (for starred segment matching)
- laps (for lap waypoint generation)
- description and private_note (for PDF/XML export)

This method is idempotent - it only fetches data once and returns immediately on subsequent calls.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: Application context with logging

jsDoc.doc:
Adds a detailed segment to the activity.

This method will apply any configured aliases to the segment name before adding it to the activity.

tags.name: segEffort
tags.doc: The segment effort to add.

jsDoc.doc:
Attaches starred segment efforts to this activity.

This method filters the activity's segment efforts to include only those that are starred,
and applies segment name aliases (if provided in the starredSegments map).

tags.name: starredSegments
tags.doc:
Map of segment ID to (possibly aliased) segment name.
This map identifies which segments are starred and provides their names.
Number of starred segment efforts found and attached

jsDoc.doc:
A comparison function for sorting activities by their start date.

This can be used with `Array.prototype.sort()` to sort an array of activities.

tags.name: a
tags.doc: The first activity.

tags.name: b
tags.doc: The second activity.
-1 if `a` is before `b`, 1 if `a` is after `b`, and 0 if they are at the same time.

jsDoc.doc:
The main class for interacting with the Strava API.

This class provides a high-level interface for making authenticated requests to the Strava API. It handles the
authentication flow and provides methods for accessing various Strava resources.
Constructs a new `StravaApi` instance.

tags.name: userCredsFile
tags.doc: The file path or `FS.File` instance for storing user authentication tokens.

tags.name: clientCreds
tags.doc: The Strava application credentials. This can be a single `ClientCredSrc` object or an array of them. Defaults to `{ env: true }`.

jsDoc.doc:
Initializes the Strava API client by authenticating the user.

This method orchestrates the entire authentication process. It will first attempt to load existing credentials.
If the credentials have expired, it will automatically try to refresh them. If no valid credentials can be
found or refreshed, it will initiate a web-based authentication flow.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context, which includes a logger.

tags.name: opts
tags.doc: Options for initialization.

tags.name: opts.force
tags.doc: If `true`, the web-based authentication flow will be forced, even if a valid token already exists.
A promise that resolves to `true` if authentication is successful, otherwise `false`.

jsDoc.doc:
Provides access to the `StravaCreds` instance, which manages the authentication credentials.

This can be used to directly access the user's credentials, for example to get the access token or to check
the expiration date.

Retrieves the profile of the authenticated athlete.

This method will automatically handle token refreshes if necessary.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context for logging.

tags.name: athleteId
tags.doc:
The ID of the athlete to retrieve. If not provided, the currently authenticated athlete's
profile will be returned.
A promise that resolves to the athlete's detailed profile.

jsDoc.doc:
Retrieves a list of activities for the logged in athlete.

This method will automatically handle token refreshes if necessary.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context for logging.

tags.name: options
tags.doc: Options for retrieving activities.
A promise that resolves to an array of activities.

jsDoc.doc:
Retrieves the authenticated athlete's starred segments.

This method recursively fetches all pages of starred segments and accumulates them into the provided array.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context for logging.

tags.name: accum
tags.doc: An array to accumulate the segments into.

tags.name: page
tags.doc: The page number to retrieve. Defaults to 1.

jsDoc.doc:
Retrieves the detailed representation of a specific activity.

This method is useful for getting more detailed information about an activity than is available in the summary representation.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context for logging.

tags.name: activity
tags.doc: The summary representation of the activity.
A promise that resolves to the detailed representation of the activity.

jsDoc.doc:
Retrieves and combines stream coordinate data for a given activity or segment.

Fetches one or more stream types from the Strava API and merges them into TrackPoint objects.
Each TrackPoint represents a single point along the route with available data fields.

**Stream Types:**
- `LatLng` - Latitude/longitude coordinates (required)
- `Altitude` - Elevation in meters
- `Time` - Seconds since activity start
- `Distance` - Cumulative distance in meters
- `Heartrate`, `Cadence`, `Watts`, etc. - Additional sensor data

**Time Data:** The time stream returns seconds offset from activity start (e.g., 0, 1, 2...).
Use `Activity.startDateEx(seconds)` to convert to timezone-aware timestamps.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: Application context for logging

tags.name: source
tags.doc: Type of object ('activities' or 'segments')

tags.name: streamTypes
tags.doc: Array of stream types to retrieve and merge

tags.name: objId
tags.doc: ID of the activity or segment

tags.name: name
tags.doc: Name of the object (for logging)
Array of TrackPoint objects with requested stream data merged

jsDoc.doc:
Retrieves the stream data for a given activity or segment.

Streams are the raw data associated with an activity or segment, such as latitude/longitude, heart rate, or power.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context for logging.

tags.name: source
tags.doc: The type of object to retrieve the stream for (e.g., 'activities', 'segments').

tags.name: objId
tags.doc: The ID of the activity or segment.

tags.name: options
tags.doc: Additional query parameters for the request.

A promise that resolves to a dictionary of stream data, where the keys are the stream types and the values
are arrays of the stream data.

jsDoc.doc: Retrieves a specific segment.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context for logging.

tags.name: segmentId
tags.doc: The ID of the segment to retrieve.
A promise that resolves to the segment data.

jsDoc.doc: Retrieves the efforts for a specific segment.

tags.name: ctx
tags.doc: The application context for logging.

tags.name: segmentId
tags.doc: The ID of the segment.

tags.name: params
tags.doc: Additional query parameters for the request.
A promise that resolves to an array of segment efforts.

jsDoc.doc:
Checks if the provided data is a valid `StravaCredsData` object.

This is a type guard that checks if the object has the required properties and that the `token_type` is 'Bearer'.

tags.name: val
tags.doc: The data to validate.
`true` if the data is a valid `StravaCredsData` object, `false` otherwise.

jsDoc.doc:
Manages Strava API credentials, including reading, writing, and validating them.

This class provides a convenient way to work with Strava credentials, abstracting away the details of file I/O and
validation.
Constructs a new `StravaCreds` instance.

tags.name: tokenFile
tags.doc:
The file path or `FS.File` instance for storing the credentials. This file will be used to
persist the credentials across sessions.

jsDoc.doc: The expiration timestamp of the access token in epoch seconds.
The refresh token used to obtain a new access token.
The access token used to make API requests.
The file path where the credentials are stored.

Checks if the credentials object contains a non-empty refresh token.

This is useful for determining if the credentials can be refreshed.

tags.doc: `true` if a refresh token is present and not empty, `false` otherwise.

jsDoc.doc:
Checks if the access token is valid and has not expired.

An access token is considered valid if it has not expired and its `token_type` is 'Bearer'.

tags.name: t
tags.doc: A buffer time in seconds. The token will be considered expired if it expires within this buffer time.

tags.name: now
tags.doc: The current time in epoch seconds.
`true` if the token is valid, `false` otherwise.

jsDoc.doc:
Checks if the access token needs to be refreshed.

An access token needs to be refreshed if it is not valid or if it will expire within the specified refresh window.

tags.name: t
tags.doc: The refresh window in seconds. Defaults to 30 minutes.

tags.name: now
tags.doc: The current time in epoch seconds.
`true` if the token needs to be refreshed, `false` otherwise.

jsDoc.doc:
Reads the credentials from the file system.

If the file does not exist, this method will do nothing. If the file exists but contains invalid data, it will throw
an error.

tags.doc: A promise that resolves to the credentials data if the file is read successfully, otherwise `undefined`.
If the credentials file contains invalid data.

jsDoc.doc:
Writes the credentials to the file system.

This method will overwrite the existing file if it exists.

tags.name: data
tags.doc: The credentials data to write.
If the provided data is invalid.

jsDoc.doc:
Type guard to check if a value is a valid StravaId.

StravaIds are JavaScript integers. While Strava's API uses int64 (Long) values,
in practice these values are small enough to fit within JavaScript's Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER.

Type guard to check if a value is a valid Stream object.

A Stream has a type property and a data array.
Note: When key_by_type is used in Strava API, the type field may be omitted
(it's implicit from the object key), so we only require the data array.

Type guard to check if a value is an array of Stream objects.

This is the format returned when key_by_type parameter is empty/false.

Type guard to check if a value is a StreamSet object.

This is the format returned when key_by_type=true.

Type guard to check if a StreamSet has valid latlng data.

Checks if the StreamSet has a latlng property with a data array.

Type guard to check if a value is a SummarySegment.

IDs are validated as StravaId (JavaScript integers) per Strava API Long type specification.
Type guard to check if a value is an array of SummarySegment objects.
Type guard to check if a value is an array of DetailedSegmentEffort objects.

Type guard to check if a value is a DetailedAthlete.

IDs are validated as StravaId (JavaScript integers) per Strava API Long type specification.

Type guard to check if a value is a SummaryActivity.

IDs are validated as StravaId (JavaScript integers) per Strava API Long type specification.
Type guard to check if a value is an array of SummaryActivity objects.

Type guard to check if a value is a DetailedActivity.

IDs are validated as StravaId (JavaScript integers) per Strava API Long type specification.
An authorization code obtained from the Strava OAuth2 flow.
An access token for making API requests.
A refresh token for obtaining a new access token.
A dictionary of query parameters.
Data for a track point.
Offset from start time
The client ID for a Strava application.
The client secret for a Strava application.
Strava application client credentials.
Configuration for a Strava client application.

A source for Strava client credentials.

This can be one of the following:
- An object containing the credentials directly.
- A file path to a JSON file containing the credentials.
- A boolean indicating that the credentials should be loaded from environment variables.
Options for making an authenticated API request.
Options for generating a Strava authorization URL.
Options for retrieving activities.

Data for Strava API credentials.

This is the data that is stored in the credentials file.
Data for a segment effort, as returned by the Strava API.
A segment effort.
Distance in kilometers (km). Useful for display conversions.
Latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90).
Longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180).
Distance in meters (m). Strava API uses meters for all distance values.
Time in seconds (s). Strava API uses seconds for all time durations.
A geographical coordinate, represented as a [latitude, longitude] pair.
A geographical rectangle defined by two corner points.
Check if a value is a valid Strava ID (integer).
Check if a value is a valid Activity ID.
Check if a value is a valid Athlete ID.
Check if a value is a valid Segment ID.
Check if a value is a valid Gear ID (string).

Check if a value is a valid ActivityType.
Also accepts any string (for custom activity types).
Check if a value is a known/standard ActivityType.
Check if a value is a valid Sex value.
Check if a value is a valid ResourceState.
Check if a value is a valid SportType.
Check if a value is a valid StreamType.
Check if a value is a valid UnitSystem.
Check if a value is a plain object (not null, not array).
Check if a value is a valid polyline map.
Check if a value is a valid MetaAthlete.

Check if a value is a SummaryActivity.
Validates only critical fields: id, name, distance.

Check if a value is a DetailedActivity.
Validates critical fields: id, name, distance, calories.
Check if a value is an array of SummaryActivity objects.
Check if a value is an array of DetailedActivity objects.
Check if a value is a Lap.
Check if a value is a Split.
Check if a value is a PhotoSummary.

Check if a value is a SummaryAthlete.
Validates critical fields: id, firstname, lastname.

Check if a value is a DetailedAthlete.
Validates critical fields and required DetailedAthlete fields.
Check if a value is an array of SummaryAthlete objects.
Check if a value is a SummaryClub.

Check if a value is a SummarySegment.
Validates critical fields: id, name, distance.
Check if a value is a DetailedSegment.
Check if a value is an array of SummarySegment objects.

Check if a value is a SummarySegmentEffort.
Validates critical fields: id, elapsed_time, distance.
Check if a value is a DetailedSegmentEffort.
Check if a value is an array of DetailedSegmentEffort objects.
Check if a value is an ExplorerSegment.

Check if a value is a DataStream.
Validates critical fields: type, data array.

Check if a value is a LatLngStream.
Validates critical fields: type is 'latlng', data is array of [lat, lng] pairs.
Check if a value is a Stream (either DataStream or LatLngStream).

Check if a value is a StreamSet.
Validates that it's an object with potential stream properties.
Check if a StreamSet has valid latlng data.
